leadershipRequirements: Own technical direction for all web projects
Build and grow web engineering team
Enable non-engineers to ship software safely
summary: Lead Web Engineer role at Genki, a profitable 25-person remote startup building health insurance for digital nomads. The role requires end-to-end ownership of web technical direction, starting as the sole web engineer with team growth expected. Candidates must have production web application experience, cross-project infrastructure expertise, AI-agentic development proficiency (Claude Code/Codex), and the ability to enable non-engineers to safely ship software. Current stack includes TypeScript, React, Astro, Vite, GraphQL, Cloudflare, and Railway. Compensation up to 10,000 EUR/month with equity.

prioritySignals.reason: Explicitly stated as core requirement; must use Claude Code or similar daily and know how to steer AI agents effectively
Must have shipped production apps from static sites to complex SPAs and understand real-world problems
Need shared libraries, CI/CD, monorepo, deployment automation experience—not just solo development
Starting as sole web engineer with team growing around; must own technical direction and mentor
Health insurance domain requires strong security; production systems mindset is essential
Must unify organically grown codebase and build foundation; includes enabling non-engineers to contribute
Fully remote team across Europe and Southeast Asia; needs clear written communication without excessive meetings
